commands.description: Load Effective Address: Calculates the memory address using scaled indexing, useful for array access without arithmetic overhead.
Shift Logical Left: Shifts bits to the left, effectively multiplying the value by powers of two; useful for high-performance bit manipulation.
Logical Compare: Performs a bitwise AND without modifying the destination, commonly used to check if a register is zero by setting the Zero Flag.
Repeat Move String Byte: A high-performance instruction used in loops to copy blocks of memory rapidly.
Software Interrupt/System Call: Triggers a transition from user mode to kernel mode to request OS services (e.g., file I/O, process exit).
